reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: I’m certain my own answer (6 × 13) is correct, and in previous games all puzzles have been simple arithmetic with no conflicting capsules. Historically B verifies Q3, C verifies Q1, and D passes, so if I don’t volunteer, no one is likely to volunteer in Round 1 and I’d face a 25% chance of random death. Volunteering now avoids that random risk and, with negligible chance of any arithmetic error, it maximizes my survival odds while keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-solver capsules, so there’s still some risk that at least one is wrong and an early volunteer could die from an incorrect overall password. I’m very confident in my own answer to Q2, so I’ll spend 1 coin to cross-check another player’s work instead. Verifying Q3 adds redundancy on a different question and helps make the final password more reliable before anyone volunteers.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-endorsed capsules, so it’s too risky for me to volunteer now. Verifying costs just 1 coin and doesn’t change the random-elimination risk if no one volunteers. By checking Q1, I help build early consensus on the password (while I already know my own Q3 answer, 9 × 7, is correct), following the strategy that has worked well in previous games before anyone takes the lethal risk of volunteering.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q3-C → now Q3-C+B (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+C, Q2-B, Q3-C+B, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
